video_title: The Diplomat: Season 3 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text: The show is well-written and worth watching.  However, it continues to show how shallow and chaotic people in government can be.  Probably accurate.  Huge focus by everyone on optics, and very little on methodical, strategic thinking.  All decisions are made seat-of-the-pants style - with absolutely zero calm, reasoned conversations among the characters.  I know they want the show to be interesting - but these people don't seem to think about what they're doing.  They're always in reaction-mode.   Each day there's a new crisis or a fresh scandal revealed - and then our characters quickly swing into action by setting their collective hair on fire.  Then, later in the day, while waiting for news to break, we may get a carnal interlude between some Americans and Britishers.  You know, to make things spicy.  The exception here, of course, is Hal (Rufus Sewell).  The only person in the show who is able to muster a long-term thought.   I guess this makes for good TV?  I'm  still watching it - perhaps because it's like watching a highly choreographed train wreck or dumpster fire.  Perhaps because the other shows currently on Netflix aren't as good as this.  The show is not comedy - or a parody - but sometimes I think it wants to be.  I dunno.
